 P. 78. त्रासगद्गदं— in faltering accents through fear. अर्यवर्य— The prince of merchants. अर्यः is fr. 1 P. to go, +य (यत्); ऋ takes यत् in the sense of 'a maater or Vaiśya;' otherwisa it will take ण्यत्; as आर्यः ब्राह्मणः अन्वजानात्— consented to give in marriage, betrothed. स पुनः— सः goes with—मे पिता. दरिद्रति—Loc.Abc. श्लाध्यनामन् The praiseworthy epithet वरयत्येव तस्मिन्— When he was quite willing to marry me. साथवाह— the leader of merchants trading with foreign countries. ०दत्तसङ्केता– See com; also, प्रियतमाय दत्तः सङ्केतो यया, having given an intimation to.' मन्मथाभिसरा— guided by Oupid. अभिसरामि— simply means ' go to' it has not got here its technical sense of 'going for enjoyment.' *Cf* Bhûsbaṇâ— नेयमभिसारिका। तादृशरतिसुखेच्छयाऽभिसरणाभावात्। 'रतार्थिनी तु सकेतं याति या साऽभिसारिका' इति रसात्नहार। भाण्डं —a collection of ornaments. नागरिकवल— party of polic-man, the city watch. मृदु– gentle, happy. वयमिमां— Here वयं stands for आवां; see com. which quotes Pán. I 2. 59. एकत्वे द्वित्वं च विवक्षिते अस्मदो बहुचतं वा स्यात्। वयं ब्रूमः। पक्षे अहं ब्रवीमि, आवां व इति वा । Sid.– Kau. भावित॰—affected with the pangs of venom spreading through my body. दर्वीकर— a snake दर्वी दर्व्याकारः फणा एव करा यस्य प्रहारसाधनत्वात् । दर्वी फणां करोति इति वा।

 P. 79.बाष्पदुर्दिनाक्षी— Her eyes shedding a profusion of tears. नरेन्द्राभिमानी— Proud of his being an adept in poisontreatment. मुद्रा— a particular shape given to the forehand by intertwining the fingers, at the time of worshipping, or propitiating a deity, &c.; *cf.* योजनात्सर्वदेवानां द्रावणात्पापसंहतेः। तस्मान्मुद्रेति सा ख्याता सर्वकामार्थसाधनी॥ इति तन्त्रसारः. This had the same effect as the magnetic passes of modern hypnotism have. magical and mysterious formularies for the propitia tion of deities or the attainment of superhuman power (as opposed to the repetition of spells). उपक्रम्य— having treated me तन्त्र— bitten by Death me. अकृतार्थ—being unsuccessful. कालदष्ट—bitten by Death or by a deadly snake. स्तब्धश्याव—stiff and darkened. रुद्धा—fixed glazed. अतिवर्तते— goes beyond,

 पाटितध्वान्त॰— which pierced the darkness by the stream of its splendour. वाक्पुनः &c.— Mark the play on the words दत्ता and अपहृता. You have given me my beloved, but deprived me of the power of speech;' *i.e*. I am so much overwhelmed with satisfaction and wonder at this your conduct that I can find no words to express myself. According to the Bhushaṇâ the rhetorical figure is परिवृत्ति hore. त्वत्कर्म &c.— There seems to be some confusion here in the text. The न after इदं found in many editions and adopted by Bühler[^907] is not in keeping with your character, it appears marvellous, no other (thief) has acted thun before you,' (it might be objected) that the power of things &c.") does not yield a good sense. The Bhushaṇâ also seems to read the same. We adopt the reading ननु for a, reading वक्तुम्।... अद्भुतमिति। इदं ननु ते &c. Now Udâraka explains how he is unable to himself; proposes one explanation which ends with इति; but the next clause shows the impropriety of this. This constitutes the rhetorical figure आक्षेप. Thus he says—(If I were to say) this your act (*viz.* your escorting this damsel to me and restoring the ornaments) is wonderful (then it might be urged) that your character, indeed, appears to be marvellous; and so there is nothing extraordinary in this; it is a thing that can be naturally expected from such a person.' प्रतिनियतैव वस्तु॰— He means that each individual is made of different stuff, and therefore exhibits only those qualities which are peculiar to

[^907]: # "For, I do not know how to describe this wonderful deed of yours. (If I were to say, 'This deed

—————————————————————————————————————————

 "For, I do not know how to describe this wonderful deed of yours. (If I were to say, 'This deed) is not in keeping with your character, it appears marvellous, no other (thief) has acted thun before you,' (it might be objected) that the power of things &c." (Bühler's tr.). Here the आक्षेप is not properly brought out.

or inherent in its nature. So others in whom covetousness and such other vices are found may not do it. But in you, they are absent; so there is no point in saying that no one else has done so.

 P. 80. त्वयाऽद्य &c.— (If I were to say)—'To-day you have shown what saintly character is, that would not do; since most your previous acts must have been of such nature (and so I might offend you).' Bühler translates—"If I were to say– ‘You have shown yourself to-day a saint', (that description' would be wrong because) it does not agree with your acts performed in former births, *i.e*. your generosity shows that in former births you must have performed many meritorious acts; because you could not act so generously if you had not done so; hence you cannot be said to have begun a course of saintliness *to-day*." But this is far-fetched.

 त्वदाशयं &c.—'It will not be reasonable to arrive at such a decision without having correctly estimated (or, formed a proper notion about) your noble nature (for you might have done nobler acts previously).' आशय may also mean ‘opinion.' असारं—something worthless or unsubstantial, *i.e.* his own self. अतिगरीयसा— by a heavy price (obligation). इति स ते प्रज्ञा॰— It will be an insult to your high mental powers (for it would mean you do not know how to make a purchase). तदलाभे— had I not obtained my beloved (this body should have perished). So त्वयैव दत्तं— my body is virtually a gift from you. प्राप्तरूपं— excellent for the occasion; fit for me to say. See com. प्रतिपत्तिः —course, way of action; 'what course of conduct do you mean to adopt now ?' जिहासामि— I wish to leave स्वदेशः &c — *Cf*. को वीरस्य मनीषिणः स्वविषयः को वा विदेशस्तथा। Hit. I प्रत्यवायभूयिष्ठ— beset with dangers. शैथिल्य – deäorency. Such an abandonment of the native place bespeaks something like want of talent and spirit (mental calibre), on one's part. अविचारानुमतेन = अविचारकृतमनुमतं यस्य तेन; an awkward comp., if not positively wrong; 'who assented without any more thought,' *i. e.* readily. अपसर्पभूतया— Who served as our spy or guide. मृद्भाण्डावशेषं—Leaving (*i.e.* everything except) the earthen vessels. नागरिकसम्पाते— as we encountered a party of the town-police.

 P. 81. ग्रैवेय—ग्रीवायां भवं ग्रैवेयं by adding ढञ् (एय) to ग्रीवा; the neck-chain. पातिता॰—Who stooped to strike a slanting blow with his tusk on the broad chest of the driver that was thrown down. For परिणत see com.; and *of* वप्रक्रीडापरिणतगजप्रेक्षणीयं ददर्श Megh. I. 2; also Śiś. IV. 29, Kir. VI. 7. पुरीतत्—An intestine With his tusk encircled near the heart with his entrails. अक्षिणोत्—destroyed (the city-guard ). शाखाग्राहिकया — by catching hold of the branches of a tree (and holding to them till the animal passed on).

 पद्मरागशृङ्ग—A peak formed of rubies. Dissolve उदयाचलेन्द्रस्य पद्मराग॰ आपीड—a cluster. पतङ्ग— the sun. प्रगेतन— to be performed in the morning. तन is added to adverbs of time such as सायं (*cf* सायन्तने सवनकर्मणि Śak. III. 24 ), चिरं, प्रगे &c. in the sense of of that time;' see Pâṇ. IV. 3. 23. अस्मत्कर्मतुमुलं— astir on account of our deeds वधूगृहेषु॰— The pl may bo accounted for by supposing that the word used here is गृह *m*. and *pl.* (the house with its environs). कुलपालिका— name of Kuberadatta's daughter. उपह्वरे— in secret. चर्मरत्न॰— This bag of excellent leather; or, handsome leather-bag; or, a leather-bag for holding gems. मूलहरत्वमेत्य— come to be deprived of my paternal wealth or my principal stock of wealth. The other sense given by the Bhushaṇâ does not seem to be good. अर्थिवर्गात्— to be construed with एत्य.

 P. 82. उपनगर॰— उपनगरं भवम् उपनगरभवम्; सुप्सुपोते समासः। कण्ठन्यास्त॰—when about to place the weapon on my neck; here the p. p. has the sense of 'immediate futurity' by the Vàrttika आदिकर्मणि निष्ठा वक्तव्या. अवज्ञासोदर्यं – The sister of contempt; *i.e* which exposes one to constant humiliation. आत्मत्याग— suicide. आत्मना—by personal efforts; construe this with उद्धरन्ति. *Cf*. उद्धरदात्मनाऽऽत्नानम् Bg. VI 5. प्रतिसन्धान—rejoining मन्त्रसिद्धः— pussessing the power of spell, rendered effisavious. मन्त्रेषु सिद्धः, or मन्त्राः सिद्धाः यस्य. the comp. being taken as one of the आहिताग्न्यादि class. साधिता—endowed with magical power or properties. लक्षग्राहिणी—holoing or granting lacs of coins by being operated upon by *mantras*. कामरूप— Name of a country which extended from the banks of the Karatoyá or Sadánīrá to the extremities of Assam. The ancient capital of the kingdom was Prâgjyotisha. See our note on Ragh. IV. 81 मत्सरिणी— exposed to jealousy. मदन्यत्र— excepting me. दुग्धे—yields treasure, fulfils desires. किं तु &c— But on this condition, that one must first restore whatever one might have dishonestly got from others, &c.अथ— And after that. स एष कल्पः— This is the prescribed way of making it grant one's object. ग्रावच्छिद्रं— a cave (*lit*, a cavity in rocks or a mountain ).

 P. 83. यथोक्तं— As said before (to the king). वरिवस्यमाना— Prep. p. of the pass of वरिवस्यति, a, Denom. verb from वरिवस् a word meaning 'worship. serve formed by adding क्यच् (य) by नमोवरिवश्चित्रङ क्यच्। Pán. III. 1. 19; *lit*, being waited upon or worshipped for money; hence, being emptied by daily demands. व्यवहर्तुम्— To seek redres by legal means; to proceed legally against him मन्त्रियोगात्— At my command; this may also be construcd wish वैरमवर्धयत् विमर्दकः— The gambler whom Apahár, assisted; see p 77 उदारके—The Student will remember that उदारक is another name of Dhanamitra. प्रत्यचध्नात्—came in the way; opposed the match.

 पञ्चवीरगाष्ठ— The public hall or concert hall. पञ्चवीर probably means the chief people of the four classes and the Nishádas &c. forming the fifth class; hence, all classes of people. सान्द्रादरः— Full of eager curiosity. तद्दृष्टी &. —The reading current in most editions is सत्रा॰ (instead of सच्चापा॰); it is not easy of explanation. One Com. explains it as having no other refuge (अपगतः आश्रयः — *i e*. अन्य आश्रय — यस्य) than the covering (सत्र=shield) of the lotus-bed of her amorous or coquettish glances;' for the comparison of glances to lotuses comp. not, on अपाङ्गदाम p. 74, and लीलाकटाक्षमाला further on. Or the comp may be taken as made up of two Bah. s; ०वनमेव सत्रं यस्य स ०वनसत्रः; अपगत आश्रयो यस्य सः अपाश्रयः and then ०वनसत्रः चासौ अपाश्रयश्च; 'one who shielded himself with, had no other support than,' &c. The ultimate sense in either case is that her glances were maddening. A third way is to take the comp. as सत्रमेव अपाश्रयो यस्य; अपाश्रय itself is often used in the sense of आश्रय; *cf* कर्णे कृतापाश्रयः Veṇ. V. 1. It is pretty certain that the reading सत्रा॰ is a mislection for the original सच्चा॰ which is found in the Calcutta editions, and which means "supported by his excellent bow in the form of the lotus-bed of her sportive glances &c." This yields a readily-understood sense and is free from awkward compounds.

 P. 84. भावा— feelings रसाः— sentiments; or the Bhávas intensified; see com सामग्र्य – full display. समुदितबल— having gained force. नगरदेवतेव &c—has occurred before; see p. 77. Here it is out of place. लीलाकटाक्ष॰ — लीलायुक्ता कटाक्षा लीलाकटाक्षास्तेषां माला एव शृङ्खलास्ताभिः। कटाक्षः— कटौ अतिशयितौ अक्षिणी यत्र, अक्षि being changed to अक्ष by बहुव्रीहौ सक्थ्यक्ष्णोः &c. Páṇ. V. 4. 113; or कट गण्डम् अक्षति इति, अण् (अ) being added कमणि, to अक्ष नृत्य— is a dance accompanied by a pantomimic display of feelings (अन्यद्भावाश्रयं नृत्यम् Daśarúpaka) and is different from, नृत्त, which is a dance to the accompaniment of music and the beatings of hands (ताल) but witheut gesticulations. सिद्धिलाभशोभिनी— appearing more graceful, or with her beauty heightened, by the (flush of) success. किं विलासात्—whether through coquetry (natural to her) सविभ्रमारेचित॰— with her bushy eye-brows sportively or gracefully bent. For आरेचित *cf* Kum. III. 5. सापदेशं—under some pretext. सोऽहं—I thus conditioned, *i. e*. who was thus lovingly looked at by her. दुर्निवारयोत्कण्ठया—with a longing that I could not check. दूरीकृता॰— not feeling disposed to take food. मुक्तैरवयवैः— with drooping limbs. निष्णातः—*Lit*. one who dives to the bottom; hence, secondarily, one who knows fully everythin about a thing. The स् of स्न is changed to व् by Pâṇ VIII. 3. 89 (see com. ) when the sense is 'skill in. भाववृत्तिः—The love-affected state of her mind. शरशयने—on a bed of arrows; *i. e.* he will discharge so many arrows at her, piercing her body, that when she will lie down she will sleep on a bed of them as it were. स्थानाभिनिवेशिनोः— who have fxed your affections each on the proper person.

 P. 85. गणिकास्वधर्म॰ — contrary to the proper duty of courtesans. भद्रोदारेणाशयेन—with good and noble intentions. गुणशुल्का न धनशुल्का— I am to be won by merit, not by wealth (*lit.* the price for my hand is merits and not wealth). प्रतिषिध्याकृतार्था — not having succeeded in dissuading her from her purpose. मूलच्छिन्न—nipped in the bud. अच्युतं—branded or blameless, chaste, virtuous. It may also be taken as an adv. ' so as not to fall off from it,' rigidly. अनाश्रवा—*fem* 'disobedient' आशृणोतीति आश्रवः one who obeys; fr. श्रु with आ + अ (अच्) by पचाद्यच्। Pâṇ. III. 1. 134. आवर्ज्य— having won her or secured her affections. प्रधानदूती— chief agent, principal messenger. शाक्यभिक्षुकी— A Buddhist female mendicant or nun. चीवर—old garments. पिण्ड— food. बन्धकी— (that) harlot (Kâmamañjarî). अजिनरत्न—The gom of, *i. e.* the wonderful bag.

 P. 86. सम्प्रतिपन्नायां— when she agreed to my proposal. करकिसलय—her sprout-like *i. e*. tender hand. *Cf.* Uttar. III. 39; Rit. VI. 30. चर्मरत्नस्तेयवादः &c. —The mention of the theft of the wonderful bag was to be made, *i. e.* on which the theft was to be announced as having taken place. मत्प्रणिधिः— my secret emissary. अर्थपतिगृह्यः―a partisan or friend of Arthapati.नाम― is here used अलीके; to all appear; *cf.* परिश्रमं नाम विनीय च क्षणम्। Kum. V. 32. उल्लङ्ध्य—having insulted. बह्वतर्जयत्— spoke many an angry word; used threatening language. आक्रुश— to revile. स एष धनगर्वो नाम—It is your well-known pride of wealth. परस्य भार्यां &c.—This refers to Kulapálikâ whom her father Kuberadatta first proposed to give in marriage to Arthapati, but afterwards wished to bestow on Dhanamitra. प्रतीत—well-known. बहिश्चराः प्राणाः— *i.e.* his second self. ब्रह्महत्यामपि &c.—I will make no scruple to kill even a Bráhmaṇa (if there be need). एकरात्रजागर &c.— It needs only one night's wakefulness on my part to remove this burning fever of your pride &c. He means that by keeping awake one night he would be able to steal his bag and humble his pride. सामर्षम्—Angrily. कृत्रिमार्तिना —who feigned to be uneasy, who wore a look of painfulness. वेशवाट— the street lined with the residences of courtesans. निपुणम्— carefully.

 P. 87. the उपलभ्येत—उपलभ्यते *v.l* is not good for in this case स must be taken as referring to Vimardaka, which does not follow syntactically. स वराकः— The poor fellow, *i.e.* (Arthapati). स्वदभिज्ञान॰—त्वत् refers to Râjaváhana to whom Apahâra is narrating his story. प्रत्याख्याय—having denied the charge; supply स्थितः after this to make the context clear. विभाविते—The matter being proved or made clear (to the king). कल्पोक्तेन— according to the prescribed mode of using it. सप्रश्रयं— humbly. निर्ग्रन्थिकग्रह— The dogmas of Jainism, the Jaina cult (com.). ग्रह rather means 'the grip' (power), or clutches of the Jainas. निर्ग्रन्थिक properly signifies 'one having no knotŚ (of the crest, garments &c. ); hence, a Jaina mendicant of the Digambara class. अश्मन्तक—the hearth, *i.e.* she gave away every valuable in her house. अश्मन्तक is derived as अश्मनोऽप्यन्तोऽत्र, where even a stone is destroyed; a word of the शकन्ध्वादि group ( tho Saṃdhi being पररूप). अभ्युदय— prosperity. मिथ— secretly. लोकपक्रोशपात्रं— the object of the derision of, *i.e*. who has earned from the people the nickname of. निरपेक्षं—-with indifference. चर्मरत्नलाभहेतुः—It must be due to her acquisition of the magical purse. कल्प— see p 82. व्यथितवर्णेनेव—with apparent fear pr concern; looking as if agitated.

 P. 83.अतिप्रकाशत्— which has been widely known. तदनुयोगाय– to question you about that. निर्बद्धया— when pressed. तदागतित्वेन— (तस्य आगतिस्तस्य भावस्तेन) as the source of its acquisition. अहमपदेश्यः— you will certainly point to me, *i. e.* disclose my name. चित्रवधः— death with tortures. अनर्थानुबन्धिनी— has in its train, would be followed by, a series of calamities. तदपयशः—the infamy about the purse being stolen. कीनाशः—the niggardly fellow or cheat. अभ्युपगमय्य— having thus communicated with me. नैष न्यायः— It is not a proper course of not behove. अतिप्रणुद्य— having urged this with great force. The Com says, 'having concealed,' *i. e.
*refused to give out the name. उपक्षेप— the mention or suggestion of दग्धबन्धकीभ्यां— by those dastardly girls.

 P. 89. आर्य— is rather a strange term of address for a king. Some propose to read आर्यमौर्यदत्त as one word. There is also a suggestion to take आर्य in this comp. as referring to Châṇakya, and मौर्य to Chandragupta. But, as remarked by Mr. Agashe, आर्य though usually prefixed to the name of Cháṇakya can hardly be used alone to stand for it. It is better to read देव for आर्य which is found in a Southern MS. मौर्यदत्तः— Granted by Maurya (or the Maurya kings); Maurya is probably Chandragupta (See p. 191 *infra*) whose reign begins in 315 B C. and for whose guidance a cude of law was drawn up by Cháṇakya. वर—a boon, special privilege. But Chandragupta is not known to have exempted the merchant-class from capital punishment for such offences; nor is there anything to this effect in Obâṇakya's code Mr. Agashe, however, says— “The only authority that can be found in the Kauṭiīya for immunity from capital punishment of the Vaiśyas as a class in contained in the following couplet:— एवं चोरानचोराख्यान् वणिक्कारुकुशीलवान्। भिक्षुकान्कुहकाश्चान्यान्वारयेद्देशपीडनात्॥ Mysore Series. " भर्ता—The king पटच्चर—a rag, अर्थमत्त— vain of his wealth. अपविद्ध— given away. अभिनोदितेन &c. — *i. e.* at Dhanamitra's instance. गुणिनि—auspicious. सिद्धसङ्कल्पः— whose plans succeeded or bure their fruit. लुब्ध॰— The wealthy misers, the miserly sons of the wealthy. कपालपाणिः— with broken platters in their hands. नियतिलिखिता लेखा— *Lit* the line drawn by Destiny; what is fated. प्रणयकोप—. Love-anger. प्रणयसमर्पित— *i. e* by Rágam; of. for the idia ददौ रसात्पङ्कजरेणुगन्धि गजाय गण्ड्षजलं करेणुः। Kum. III 37. मदेनास्पृश्ये— I got intoxicated or tipy. मदोन्नादयोः— of drunkenness and over- excitement (or delirium) For this distinction between मद and उन्माद, *cf.* आचार्येण वृकोदरस्य हलिनोन्मत्तेन मत्तेन वा । Veṇ VI, 27,

  P. 90. प्रव्यथित— Alarmed. ०अतिवर्ती— Turning & deaf ear to, disregarding. नातिपरिकरः— not well-equipped, अवसन्न— enfeebled by. आर्तरव— cries of distress. बोधितः— brought back to my senses. प्रतिभा— ready wit; it is defined in poetics as 'talent shining with ever-new ideas or flashes of imagination; see com. ; this sense, however, is not intended here. व्यचीचरम्— mused, pondered within myself. प्रोर्णुतौ—P. p. p. of ऊर्णु with प्र; *lit*. 'covered with;' hence, involved in. प्रतिपत्ति— the course or plan to be adopted. The word occurs again at P. 98. जरतिका— an old hag. तां—The notorious one.

 P. 91. साराभरण—precious ornaments. निःशल्य— free from a dart, easy (for I have accomplished what I had purposed to do for taking vengeance on you). उद्धटितज्ञा—who knew was meant to be suggested, *i. e.* how to take a hint. सामपूर्वम्—having first addressed them in conciliatory tones. भद्रकाः— Good sirs. एकमपराधं— her bringing about the union of Rágamañjari with Dhanamitra, कलत्राभिमर्शी— the violator or ravisher of (your) wife. आकल्पसारः &c. — Courtesans entirely depend upon their decorations (dress and ornaments), *i. e.* by their means alone they can attract others. रूपाजीवा—*Lit*, 'one who gains her living by her beauty;' रूपम् आजीवो यस्याः. वैरानुबन्ध— inveterate enmity प्रतिपन्नार्थेव— seeming to have understood or grasped my meaning. भद्रमुखः— a good fellow; *lit.* having a beautiful or auspicious countenance. It is used as an honorific title of address like, प्रियदर्शन, but in the case of ordinary men alone. In dramas a prince is to be addressed by the low characters; see Sáh. -D. VI. 154 आरक्षिकनायक—-The chief of the city-guards; head constable. चारक— prison.

  P. 92. उत्तरेद्युः— उत्तरस्मिन् अहनि; अहन is changed to एद्युस when proceded by पूर्व, अन्य, अपर, उत्तर &c; see Pán. V. 3. 28. दृप्ततरः— highly conceited. सुभगमानी— who considered himself very fortunate. सुन्दरम्मन्य —Kántaka thought that he was loved by women (सुभग) and so considered himself beautiful. see *supra*, notes on सुभगपताका, and सुभगम्मन्यः P. 74. अत्यय— death. अनतिपक्व— not much grown wiser by experience. नागरिक— has occurred before in the sense of 'policemen' or city guards; see p. 78, 80 &c. ; here it means 'the chief of police men, or magistrate, who it seems had to look after jails or to act as a jailor also; *cf* for this sense ततः प्रविशति नागरिकः श्यालः &c. Sák. VI. p. 138, Vik. V. p. 117. Further on it means 'citizens.’ कारणा— crucial or agonizing pain, tortures. मित्रमुखे—having the face of, *i.e*. an outward (false) friend. प्रत्याशा— hope to regain. साधीयसी सन्धा— firm resolve. तर्जन— threatening. वर्तमाने अनुयोगव्यतिकरे— While I was being continually asked all manner of questions. अनुगुण—proper, wholesome. विरोपितव्रणः—with my wounds healed. अच्युताम्बर — the garment of Vishņu. हृष्टवर्ण—appearing pleased; *cf.* व्यथितवर्णेन p. 87. सुनीतिः— a wise plot, judicious plan.

 P. 93. आपन्नः— In distress. सुलभात्पानदोषात्— on account of the vice of drunkenness, so common &c. लोकवार्ता—common talk, public rumour. निकृष्टाशयः—base-hearted, mean-spirited. समुद्गक– a box. प्रणयानुवर्तिन— Acting in accordance with the love he bore to her (Râga.). उपायोपक्रान्तः—dealt with, treated with proper remedies. असुभिर्मां &c.—मा refers to Apahára., whose words, the student should remember, the nurse quoting as addressed to Dhanamitra. उपच्छन्दन—coaxing, persuasive means. अनतिवस्तु— not much alarmed at your misfortune. त्वदभिज्ञानप्रत्या॰—whose confidence was gained by showing the token of recognition from you. अन्वरञ्जयम् I pleased her (secured her favour). सङ्क्रमीकृत्य—using her as a means, through her medium.

 P. 94. प्राभृत— a present. समादधती—adjusting, putting back in its proper place. प्रच्याव्य—letting it fall, dropping it. उपकन्यापुरं— close to the princesŚ apartment. भवनाङ्गणं— the courtyard of the palace. अपदेशात् — pretending to frighten &c. प्रहसन्ती प्राहार्षम्—It was Śrigáliká's plan to lead Kântaka into the false belief that the princess was in love with him. This was the first step to entrap him. As Kántaka happened to come to the courtyard of the palace adjoining the princesŚ apartment, she threw the lotus on him under the pretext of scaring away the pigeons; of course the princess knew nothing about this. स्मयमानः— Smiling. विलासप्रप्रामाकारं— her amorous-looking सञ्ज्ञया &c.—In an indescribably clever gesticulation. दिग्धफलेन—with venomed point. अतिमुग्ध— quite beside himself. मुद्रिता— sealed पट्टांशुक, भूषणावयव— silk garments and a few ornaments. वङ्गेरिका— a cane casket. अवस्थान्तराणि— The different stages of her love-affected condition. These are mentioned as ten नयनप्रीतिः प्रथमं चित्तासङ्गस्ततोऽथ सङ्कल्पः। निद्राच्छेदस्तनुता विषयनिवृत्तिस्रपानाशः। उन्मादो मुर्छा सुतिरिन्येताः स्मरवशा वशैन स्युः॥ Ratirahasya.

 P. 95. सुदूरमुदमाद्यत—Was made to go exceedingly mad with love; his passion was carried too far. निर्माल्य— garlands worn by her the day before. अपोढानि— taken somewhere else. प्राक्षिपम् *v.l*— I threw them away. अपाविध्यं *v.l. *means the same thing—threw or cast them away; *cf.* अपविद्धसर्वस्वा *supra* p.89.* *उपमन्त्रितः — addressed. लक्षणानि &c.— The auspicious marks (of future elevation) you bear are not to be in vain or falsified. प्रातिवेश्यः— a neighbour. तदनुरूपं— in conformity with his words. समागत—united with. अयमर्थो &c.—This good fortune will be followed by another, *viz.* your acquisition of the kingdom. न आराध्यते —The subject to this is अयमर्थः; why is not this object pursued; *i.e.* why don't you adopt measures to secure the hand of the princess ? व्याम—the length of space between the tips of the fingers of both the hands when the arms are extended (Mar. वांव). आरामप्राकार—the rampart surrounding the pleasuregarden. हस्तवत्—skilful, dexterous; here हस्त means 'skill of the hand.' ऐकागारिक—एकमसहायम् अगारं प्रयोजनमस्य; he who seeks to live in a lonely or unprotected house'; hence, a thief or robber. See Pâṇ. V. 1. 113. उपरिष्टात्—after that, सगरसुतानां— The sons of Sagara (a king of the solar race), who were appointed the guardians of the sacrificial horse by their father, and who dug the earth down to the nether world in search of the horse when it was carried away by Indra in an invisible form. Here their power of digging is referred see Rám. Bála-K. chaps. 38-44; see also our note on at Ragh. III. 50.

 P. 96. अभिसन्धाय— Having come to understanding or formed an agreement with him. धार्ष्ट्यभूमिः—most intrepid. चित्रं— the same as चित्रवधेन; by subjecting him to peculiar tortures. अर्थः सिद्धति— the desired object would be gained. प्रतिपद्य— following my counsel. प्राप्तमितः परं— what is necessary to be done hereafter. त्वन्नयः— your plan or stratagem. भूयान्— of greater importance;' this is meant as a compliment to Śrigâlikâ. विनिगडीकृतः— being liberated, set free. नित्यान्धकारात्— perpetually shrouded in darkness. असस्यवाददोषेण— the oalumny of being false to my promise. निष्पततः— as I issued from the passage. असिधेन्वा— with his dagger. असिर्धेनुरिव यस्याः असिधेनुः, to which the sword stands as a cow, it being as it were its calf; *i.e.* a small sword; वा कप् असिधेनुका; this like पुत्री has a diminutive force;असिपुत्री. सन्निवेशैः— situation, position of the different rooms. मणिप्रदीपेषु— मणय एत्र प्रदीपाः the jewels serving ag powerful lamps, *i.e.* wherein gems glowed emiting light. *Cf.* ज्वलन्मणिशिखाश्चैनं वासुकिप्रमुखा निशि। स्थिरप्रदीपतामेत्य भुजङ्गाः पर्युपासते॥ Kum II. 38. महितमहार्घ &c.— the ivory feet of the shape of a recumbent lion of which were set with lawless and precious jewels. महित according to the Com. means 'big.' = properly means 'to be praised or admired,' the महितत्व lying in their size or flawlessness.

  P. 97. हंसतूलगर्भ—stufed with the downy feathers of swans. Diss. हंसतूलः गर्भे यस्यास्तादृशी शय्या उपधानं च ताभ्यां शालते इति तथा तस्मिन् कुसुमलव॰— the sides of which were decked with petals of flowers actual or engraved therein. दक्षिणपाद॰— This and the following compounds are all adverbial, moditying विश्रब्धप्रसुप्तां. अनुवलित— covered. ईषद्विवृत्त—a little turned to the side. Wilson reads विवृत— ' bare or exposed to view', which is a better reading. गुल्फ— The ankle. Diss. ईषद्विवृत्तौ (०तौ वा) मधुरौ गुल्फयोः सन्धी यथा स्यातां तथा आश्लिष्ट— in close contact. जङ्घा— The fleshy part between the ankle and the knee. ०काण्ड— *i.e.* stout and long. वेल्लित — bent (drawn in). अधिनितम्ब॰– नितम्बे इति अधिनितम्बं स्रस्तं मुक्तं च यदेकभुजलताग्रं तेन पेशलं यथा स्यात्तथा; beautiful on account of the extremity (palm) of one arm loosely thrown over the hips. अपाश्रयान्तनिमित॰—in which the other arm was contracted and thrown under the crown of the head, the palm of the tender ( sproutlike ) hand lying upstretched. ०न्तनिमित्त *v.l.*— that the extremity of the head might rest on it. अतिश्लिष्ट॰—. in which her fine undergarment of China silk lay closely adhering to her person. Fine China-silk cloth seems to have been imported into India from the earliest times, and there are numerous references to it in Sanskrit literature; *vide* sak. I. 33, Kum. VII. 3, Kir. IV 59, Mal – Mádh VI. 5, Amarn.—Ś. 75, &c. It is also mentioned in Kauṭiliya Bk. III. अनतिवलित—not much pressed down. अतनुतरनिःश्वासारम्भ— as she began to breathe perceptibly. अतिशयेन तनुः तनुतरः तथा न भवतीत्यतनुतरः (forcible ) यः निःश्वासारम्भस्तेन कम्पमानौ कुचौ कुड्मलाविव कुचकुड्मलौ यस्मिन्कर्मणि तद्यथा तथा आतिरश्चीन॰— in & manner in which the neck-ornament made of rubies and woven in the middle of her necklace of burnished gold was to be seen lying near her beautiful neck resting slantingly. अर्धलक्ष्य॰— अर्धलक्ष्यमधरकर्णपाशे निभृतं कुण्डलं यस्मिन्कर्मणि तद्यथा तथा; in which the ear-ornament lay hidden or motionless under her down-turned beautiful ear which was partly (*lit.* half) visible. अधरकर्ण may also mean 'the lower part of the ear, but the first sense is better (-see com.-) as shown by उपरिपरावृत्त in the next comp. पाश when added to कर्ण for its synonyms signifies beauty' (प्रशस्तः कर्णः कर्णपाशः).For its different meanings *cf*. Haima Kośa— पाशस्तु मृगपश्वादिबन्धने। कर्णान्ते शोभनार्थः स्यात्कचार्थे निकरार्थकः। छत्राद्यन्ते च निन्दार्थः। Kshîrasvâmî quotes a śl. bringing these meanings together— अग्रतो बाहुपाशेन केशपाशेन पृष्ठतः। पार्श्वयोः कर्णपाशेन सर्वतो बन्धनं प्रिये॥ उपरिपरावृत्त— The other beautiful ear which was turned up.

 P. 98. पिञ्जरित—tinged red. विषम॰—loosened (व्याविद्ध) braid of hair lying unevenly. उत्तराधरविवर—The interval of the upper lip (from the lower lip), *i.e.* the space between the two lips, was not to be seen, *i.e.* was covered up with the lustre of the lips. Though अधर is used for the lower lip, उत्तर is rarely used in literature for the upper lip; it is, however, so used by Kâlidâsa; *cf.* निवार्यतामालि किमप्ययं बटुः पुनर्विवक्षुः स्फुरितोत्तराधरः। a Kum. V. 83.°कर्णावतंसकृत्यं—under the her stretched hand placed broad cheek served the purpose of the ear-ornament consisting of a sprout. उपरिकपोल &c.— In which the purposе of the ornamental paintings (such 89 ladies are described to have on the particular parts of their bodies) was served by the chequered (or, embroidered) canopy mirrored into her transparent cheek that was turned upwards. विशेषक is an ornamental painting on the face of a woman variegated with dots in the middle, and drawn with such fragrant substances as rubbed sandal, musk, saffron &c.; *cf*. अभिनवा इव पत्रविशेषकाः Ragh. IX. 29; मृष्टचन्दनविशेषकभक्तिः &c. Siś. X. 84; see also Málav. III. 5. The reflection of the embroidered canopy into her cheek looked like a Viśeshaka.

 अविभ्रान्त॰— her large and bushy eyebrows were still. श्रमजलपुलक—the drops of perspiration. अलकलता — her long and curly hair. शरदम्भोधर— suggests largeness and whiteness.सोदामनीं— the simile bespeaks brightness of colour and slenderness of frame on the part of the princess.

 चोरयितव्यनिःस्पृहः— with my desire to steal totally. gone. असङ्केतित॰— touched without previous intimation (without being previously acquainted with my motive).अतिबाला—quite young (inexperienced in love-matters) व्यक्तं— undoubtedly. अहमेवाघ्नीय— I shall myself be killed, it will be all over with me. This is the meaning required by the context here. is the Pot. 3rd pers. sing. of हन् with which is A'tm. when used intransitively and with reference to one's own body. But this restriction is not always observed by the poets; *cf*. आजघ्ने विषमविलोचनस्य वक्षः Kir. XVII. 63; or आहष्वं मा रघूत्तमं Bhaṭṭi (though grammarians treat these as oversights on the part of the poots; प्रमादाः) So we may supply आत्मानं I shall ruin myself.

 P. 99. निर्यास॰— Coloured or dyed with the viscous exudation or gum (of some tree). कल्कः— a viscous preparation obtained from substances or drugs ground or pounded. वर्णवर्तिका— a drawing brush तां तथा शयानां &c.— I sketched her sleeping in that posture and myself kneeling at her feet, &c. तस्याश्च— तस्याः goes with चरणलग्न; so this is a सापेक्षसमास, चरण being connected with तस्याः (तच्चरण) वासताम्बूलवीटिका— a roll of betel-nut leaves. ०स्फुटिका— a bit or piece of . पारिजातक— scented catechu. अलक्तकपाटल— red like *alaktaka.* निरष्ठीवम्— spat out a pair of Chakravākas on the white (chunam-plastered) wall, *i.e.* produced the likeness of a pair of
Chakravák a birds by skilfully spitting on the wall. The Chakraváka bird are represented in Sanskrit literature as emblems of constancy in love; *cf.* Ragh. III. 24. Apahára.'s object in drawing a pair of these birds was to show that like the male Chakrahe too would be firm and faithful to the princess in she chose him for her husband. Now, the Chakravaka is a bird of a reddish colour; and Apahâra. produced a reddish liquid in his mouth by chewing a tâmbūla, and by gently ejecting it on the wall through his mouth, he traced on the wall a couple of the Chakravâka birds. This ho did' according to Mr. Agashe, in conformity with a diction of Vátsyayana. The following is quoted from his note. “Vàtsyâyana in his Kâmasūtra says - यत्र सम्पातोऽस्यास्तत्र चित्रकर्मणस्तद्युक्तस्य द्यर्थानां गीतवस्तुकानां क्रीडनकानां कृतचिह्नानामापीनकानामङ्गुलीयकस्य च निधानम्। प्रत्युत्तरं तया दत्तं प्रपश्येत्। Thus, according to Vatsyâyana, a lover courting a girl is recommended four devices of communicating his wish to his beloved— (1) To paint his own likeness on a board or wall (सानुरागमात्मानं फलके भित्तौ वा विलिख्य निदध्यात्, says यशोधर the commentator of कामसूत्र). This Apahár. has done. He has also addressed (2) an amorous ditty to her (गीत). The third is क्रीडनकानां पुत्रिकाकन्दुकादीनां कृतचिह्नानां निधानम्। This...seems to suggest the presentation of something suggestive of love, and hence it appears to us that Apahára. fulfilled (3) this third condition by spitting the reddish saliva on the chunam-plastered wall in such a way as to produce the likeness of a pair of Chakraváka birds... Immediately after this...comes the exchange of rings, which according to Vátsyá. is the fourth device indicative of love." नागरिकवर— one of the leading or principal citizens. उपचरित—treated as तपस्वी— poor, miserable. रहस्यं—*scil*., that he had set his heart on the princess and attempted to break into the private apartments. लब्धव्यो मोक्षः— you should procure your release. अगृह्ये— came within the reach of. अनासृष्ट—untouched.

 P. 100. स्वपृष्ठ॰— Placing my elbows on my back (and thus assuming an attitude of submission). वर्षीयसी— old hag. उन्नीत॰— having guessed what I had at heart or having divined my intention. वायुग्रस्थः— affected by hysteria or lunacy. पूर्वेद्युः— see note on उत्तरेद्युः p.92. प्रसन्नकल्पः— almost cured or freed from his malady. निष्प्रवाणि— new unbleached garments; निर्गता प्रवाणी तन्तुवायशलाका अस्मात्. औशीरे कामचारः &c.— he was allowed freely to sit or lie down, *i.e.* he was allowed liberty of action. मातरिश्वा— मातरि आकाशे श्वयति इति; the wind. शौङ्गेयः— a hawk. शान्तं पापं —*lit*. ' let the evil be averted', is used to indicate one's disapproval of what is said or heard. कदर्थिता—reproached, despised. वेशकृच्छ्रादुत्थाय— after he had escaped from the calamity brought on by the harlot. This must be connected with प्रत्यापन्न in the next comp., so that both the actions will have the same agent.

 P. 101. एवम्भूतं त्वद्दर्शनं— That I should thus obtain your sight. अपचार— misconduct, crime. निर्भिद्य— having given out or proclaimed; this should be taken with राज्ञा (निर्भिद्य प्रतिष्ठापितः), so that the use of the gerund would be correct अभियुज्य— having attacked. अमर्षण— impatient (of the outrage). पारिग्रामिकं विध— hostile measures to be adopted on besieging (परि) an enemy's town. Explain ग्रामस्य परि इति परिग्रामं Avya.; परिग्रामं (परिग्रामे वा) भवं पारिग्रामिकं by adding ठञ् (इक); see com. The hostile acts meant are such as harassing the enemy's kingdom by cutting the supplies of food, fodder, &c., breaking of tanks, &c.; these are mentioned by Manu as उपरुध्यारिमासीत राष्ट्रं चास्योपपीडयेत्। दूषयेच्चास्य सततं यवसान्नोदकेन्धनम्॥ (quoted in the Bhú as from the Mitákshará further on; see p. 134) भिन्द्याच्चैव तडागानि प्राकारपरिखास्तथा। समवस्कन्दयेच्चैनं रात्रौ वित्रासयेत्तथा॥ Manu VII 195, 196. See also Kâm. Nît. XVI. 3, 4. हठात्— forcibly. प्रतिसर— the auspicious marriage-string (the same as कौतुक; see Ragh VIII. 1). अङ्गाराजाभिसरं— coming to the aid of the King of Aṅga. सम्भूय— having joined. गतायुषः — the time of whose death had arrived. Here the त indicates near futurity; *see supra*, note on कण्ठन्यस्तशस्त्रिकः, p. 82. उपसमाधीयमान— being collected or arranged.

 P. 102. लोकसम्बाध— a throng of men coming out and going in. आथर्वणेन— by the domestic priest. A priest versed in the Atharvaṇa lore was generally selected by the ancient kings that he might perform by means of the spells given therein rites for them that would give them peculiar powers, avert calamity, put down their enemies and so on. *Cf.* Ragh. I. 59, Malli. thereon and Kâm. Nīt. IV. 32. The Atharva-Veda contains many charms and imprecations leading to good or evil results (such as bringing about the death of an enemy *&c.*). For its succint description *cf.* पुण्योऽपि भीमकर्मा निधिर्व्रतानां चकास्त्यमितशक्तिः। मूर्तिमभिरामघोरां बिभ्रादिवाथर्वणो निगमः॥ Mv. -Ch. II. 24. आदित्समान— As he wished ( *i.e.* was about) to seize. स्फुरतः —striving (to seize me). अनुविचरन्— moving from room to another. अभिनिशाम्य— seeing her before me स्तनितगम्भीरेण—*Cf * Uttar. VI, 17. A voice deep like the rumbling of clouds is considered as one of the signs (लक्षणऽ) of a great man; नाभी स्वरः सत्त्वमिति प्रशस्तं गम्भीरमेत्त्रितयं नराणाम्। उरो ललाटं वदनं च पुंसां विस्तीर्णमेतत्त्रितयं प्रशस्तम्॥ Var. Bṛih.-Saṃ. 67. 25.

 विदेहेषु — The country of Videha lay to the north-east of Magadha. Its capital Mithilâ is the same as Janakapura in Nepâl, north of Madhuvâņî. Videha must have covered, in ancient times, besides a portion of Nepâl, tho northern part of the old district of Tirhut and the north-western portion of Champaran.

 P. 103. अलिन्द— A raised place oblong in shape near a door. जैवातृकः— 'O you of long lifed' From जीव् and the (1.79 ); Uṇádi aff. आतृक (I.79);see com. ननु श्रूयते— you must heard. बलशम्बल (०र)— Names of two demons. प्रथमगंर्भा॰—when blessed with a fœtus for the first time. लेशतोऽपि &c.— what fate overtook the king of Magadha was utterly unknown. मालवेन्द्रप्रयत्न॰— who lived by the effort of the Mâlava king. This shows that Mânasara was noble—hearted. ज्येष्ठस्य— of his eldest brother. व्याप्तं— occupied, usurped. स्वस्त्रीय— his sister's son; = स्वसुः अपत्यं, by adding छ (ईय) to स्वसृ; see com. सुह्म—A country which lay to the west of Vanga or eastern Bengal. It was so called after Suhma, the fourth son of Bali. Its capital, Támralipta, also called Dâmalipta (see *infra* p. 149), has been identified with the modern Tumlook on the right bank of the Cossya, the Kapiśá of Kalidasa. For other particulars see our note on Suhma, Ragh. IV. 35. दण्डावयव— the portion of an army; for ण्ड meaning an army of. तस्य दण्डवतो दण्डः स्वदेहान्न व्यशिष्यत । Ragh. XVII. 62; प्रभवः खलु कोपदण्डयोः Kir. II. 12; see also Manu. VII. 65. लुब्धक— the wild marauders, Bhils. शार्दूल &c.— As I fell bencath the paw of a tiger. कपिला— a brown cow. क्रोड—the cavity under the bosom. इष्यसनयन्त्र— A bow.

 P. 104. मोहसुप्ता—lying in a swoon. वृष्णिपाल & shepherd. were कुटीर—a hut, a bovel. उपक्रान्तत्रणा— whose wounds dressed and tended. यावद्व्याकुलीभवामि—just as I was uneasy at heart. Cf. the account given here with that at pp. 13, 14. सार्थपाते—when the body of merchants and soldiers accom— panying them were routed. उपयन्तुं— to marry. वैक्लव्य— disgust or horror for. प्रत्याख्यानपारुष्यं— the harshness i. e. insult of a refusal. जातः— turned out to be. विष्टदोषात्— through adverseness of fate. पुनरसहिष्णुतया—and again finding his position unbearable. दग्धा— unfortunate, cursed. हतजीविताकृष्टा—out of love for her acoursed life.

 P. 105. तयोश्च सतोः— The two living. प्रसह्यकारिणः—laying violent hands on, dealing harshly with. प्रमन्युः—with her grief intensified. निगूढं— very secretly. कश्चिन्मुनिः see p. 13. अभ्युपपादनार्थं—For deliverance from distress or for protecting; according to the Bhushana 'for rearing up' (अभ्युपपादनं संवर्धनं); but this does not appear to be correct. याचितः— This is Upa.'s guess; for no request was actully made to the sage by the woman either to find out the boy or to protect him when found. उपश्लिष्य— having approached or pressed him hard. तैरपि घटन्ते॰.— Present in the sense of the Future; and the citizens might make common cause with (or join) them.' ईदृक्तया-Lit. being such and such; as to my real parentage and rank. प्रहारवर्मण्यधि- subject to his rule, under his sway. अधि is a कर्मप्रवचनीय when used in the sense of a Lord (*i. e*. being the lord of or having as lord); *e.g*. अधि भुवि रामः or अधि रामे भूः; so here अधि विदेहेषु प्रहारवर्मा also; for the Loc. see Pán. यस्मादधिकं ५०. (II.3.9) quoted in the com. प्रलम्बमान-long (of more than ordinary length). भागधेय-धेय is added स्वार्थे to रूप, नाम and भाग उपधि-a fraudulent scheme, a stratagem.

 P. 106. जालमाचरेयम् - I shall formulate a scheme for working out his destruction. मार्तण्डः - the sun; मृते अण्डे मवः। शकन्ध्यादिः; sometimes मार्ताण्ड also. ०श्वासरया॰-blown away by the force of the breath. त्रियामा-त्रयो यामा यस्याः सा, having three watches or praharas (the first and last half-prahara being discounted as being spent like a part of the day in ness - आद्यन्तयोरर्धयामयोश्चेष्टाकालत्वेन दिनप्रायत्वात्); यद्वा त्रीन् धर्मादीन् यापयति निरवकाशीकरोति (fr. या+मन् Unadi) कामप्रधानत्वात् । Bhánuji Dik. कच्चित्-asks a question to which the expected answer is 'YeŚ. I hope you know.' कामरूपेश्वरस्य - for Káma, see supra p. 82. पत्तिमभिभूय वर्तते - excels her husband (in accomplishments); or, she has not (or, acts not having) much regard for her husband, despises her husband. तदेकवल्लभः-solely devoted or attached to her. असमान॰- by finding fault with and condemning the connexion of two unequal persons. वासवदत्ता - the heroine of Subandhu's work, named after her, who was betrothed by her father to Pushpaketu but who was carried off by Kandarpaketu. Also name of the daughter of Chandamahâsena of Ujjayini (of Pradyota according to Śrî-Harshadeva; see Ratn. I. 10) who was carried off by Udayana, king of the Vatsa country. She has been referred to by Bhavabhúti in Mal-Madh. II. wherein he says she was betrothed by her father to king Samjaya, and wherein Kamandaki also makes use of a similar device to create love for Madhava and hatred for every other person in Málati's mind; of. वरेऽन्यस्मिद्वेषः पितरि विचिकित्सा च जनिता &c. II. 13. Here Dandin makes Uphár. follow Vatsyayana's suggestions; cf. परस्त्रियस्तु सूक्ष्मभावा दूतीसाध्या न तथात्मनेत्याचार्याः। Kámasů. V. 2. 1; and सैनां शीलतोऽनुप्रविश्याख्यानकपटैः सुभगङ्करणयोगैर्लोकवृत्तान्तैः कविकथाभिश्च तस्याश्च रूपविज्ञानदाक्षिण्यशीलानुप्रशंसाभिश्च तां रञ्जयेत् । कथमेवंविधायास्तवायमित्थम्भूतः पतिरिति चानुशयं ग्राहयेत् । &०. V. 4. 2, 3; शृण्वत्यां चाहल्या (Gautama's wife)- शाकुन्तलादीन्यन्यान्यपि लौकिकानि च कथयेत्तद्युक्तानि । V. 4. 14; विद्वेषं ग्राहयेत्पत्यौ रमणीयानि वर्णयेत्।...........नायकस्यानुरागं च पुनश्च रतिकौशलम्। वर्णयेत्। *Ibid*, 63, 64.

 P. 107. अनुशय— Regret, resentment, अवरोधान्तरेषु — with the other ladies of the harem, मान— jealous anger (caused by the husband's attention to a co—wife &c.); see Sáh.—Dar, III. 199, 200. अम्बा— the old woman, his foster—mother. अनन्यव्यापारया — leaving every other business. उदर्कस्वादु— having an agreeable or welcome end. अनपायिनी — never separated from her. माधवी is the spring creeper (मधौ in the spring पुष्प्यति इति; from मधु + अण + ई fem.); also called अतिमुक्त. It is generally described as twining itself round the mango—tree (sometimes round the Nipa or मधूक), but never round such trees as the Nimba even the bark of which is bitter. It becomes शोच्या if it is made to cling to a Nimba tree; hence the appropriateness of the simile. पिचुमन्द— the *nimba* tree. This is derived as पिशुं कुष्ठविशेषं मन्दयति (destroys) इति पिचुमर्द ४. 1. is also found in Amara as a variant (पिचुं मर्दयतीति). मत्तकाशिनी — belle, a beautiful woman. मत्ता इव काशते looks like one intoxicated, *i. e*. having the flush of wine on her cheeks; hence, beautiful. चित्रीयमाणा — wondering, full of astonishment; pres. p. of the denom. from चित्र by adding य (क्यच्) आश्चर्ये; चित्रीयते विस्मयते इत्यर्थः। by Pán. III. 1. 19, quoted in the com. सनाथीकृतं — Lit. is made to have a master, is graced with the handsome person of.

 P. 108. सन्निधत्ते — is present, exists in. आदृतवती — received it with respect, or eagerly. आज्ञापयसि — you say. अर्णवनेमिः— अर्णवः नेमिर्यस्याः सा the one encircled by the ocean; the earth; of. उदधिनोम Ragh. X. 10, उदधिमालामेखला Kád. p. 8 &0; for the idea comp. कालो ह्ययं निरवधिर्विपुला च पृथ्वी; Mál.—Mádh. I. 8. रूपानुरूप—रूपस्यानुरूपाणि शिल्पं च ६०. यस्य स किं लप्स्यते—is & question; 'what will he get?" सर्वमिदं &c.—All this is but too little and quite unworthy of him. (And although I give him all this, he will have obtained nothing) विप्रलम्भ — fraud, deceit. दर्शनानुभवेन — by his actual sight. निगूढं चरन्—living incognito. अन्ववर्तिष्ट — conciliated me for help in the mattor. कुसुमशेखर &c. — कुसुमानां शेखरः a chaplet worn on the crown of the head स्रक् (a garland) च अनुलेपनानि च आदी येषां तैः. This is in accordance with the precept of Vatsya. — तासां मनोहराण्युपायनानि ताम्भूलमनुलेपनम् अजमवलीयकं वासो वा तेन प्रहितं दर्शयेत्। V. 4. 35. गुणातिशय-excellent virtues. त्वत्समाधिगाढत्व - The depth of his devotion to you; (according to the Bhúshaṇá, to see the depth of your attachment to him).

 P. 109. एष चेदर्थो &c. - If this matter be determined upon, i. e. if you are really willing to deliver yourself up to him. प्राण-vital power, strength; सत्त्व mental calibre. By this she indirectly hints that in case she delivered himself up to him, he would be able to rid her of her undesirable husband. सङ्केत - appointment, engagement or place of meeting, rendezvous. दुहिता देया इत्ति-- This is in conformity with Vatsyayana's precept भवतेऽहमादौ दातुं सङ्कल्पितेत्यभिदधीत। V. 4. 30.दैवात् - such being the will of providence. नात्युपपन्नसंस्थानः-with a body not symmetrically built, not handsome in features. For this meaning of संस्थान *of*. Sák. स्त्रीसंस्थानं चाप्सरस्तीर्थमारात् &c. V. 30. कामोपचार - manner of enjoyment, love-affairs, मन्दाभिनिवेशः - not much fond of. शौर्योन्मादिन् - vain of his valour. अस्थानवर्षी - bestowing gifts on unworthy recipients; reckless in bestowing his favours. मदन्तरङ्गभूता- my confidante, confidential maid, अनात्मज्ञा - who does not know her proper position or state. नाटकीया - The dancing maid. मत्संवर्धितायाः- The selection of a particular tree and a creeper by kings and queens as their particular favourite seems to be a favourite idea with Sanskrit poets.

 P. 110. गर्भवेदिका - A dais in a cave of the pleasure- mountain. रत्नतल्प - a bedstead set with gems. अयोग्यश्च॰.- In the first place, the man is unworthy of me and now he has commenced insulting me. अनङ्गशर॰ - *Cf*. ०बाणतूणीरायमानमानसः *supra*, p. 49. अनिष्टजन॰- The misery arising from their being compelled to associate with a man they hate. अस्ति चायं ६०.- I have this treasure in my possession. By its means I will establish him in his (Vika.'s) place and live, having devoted myself entirely to him. अतः परं &c. - what is to be done next rests with you to decide. अन्तर्वंशिक॰ - See p. 56. स्थानानि their particular stations, विभागेन - as regards their different portions or subdivisions. अस्तगिरि॰- As the sun has to fall from the sky he is supposed first to jump on the peak of the setting mountain and then into the sea. क्षुभित- stirred up (and rushing to the surface). निर्वापित- extinguished. पतङ्गाङ्गार - The burning charcoal in the form of the sun. Take the comp. as a Tatp. (अङ्गारः तस्य धूमसञ्चयः) and not as a Bah, as the Com, does,

  P. 111. परामर्श - Violation, having connexion with. आचार्यकं कर्तुं--to instruct me in. आचार्यस्य कर्म आचार्यकं by affixing अक (बुञ्) which is added to words having a penultimate a pre ceded by a long syllable in the sense of भाव or कर्मन्; see Pán V. 1. 132. Cf. आचार्यकं विजयि मान्मथमाविरासीत् । Mâl. Mádh. T. 26. गुरुपरिग्रह॰- the admirer (coveter) of the wife of Brihaspati; see supra p. 70 and note. अतिराग-eager desire. स्मयमान-smiling; shining brightly. सन्धुक्षमाण॰ - whose valour was accelerated. विजिगीषा-desire for the conquest of. धर्मपीडा-(lit. 'the being constrained'), violation of religious law or righteousness. ०सन्धिना - laying to my heart, having in view. निर्हत्य- having extenuated or removed, समग्रयेत् - would endow me with; Pot. 3rd pers. sing. of the nominal verb from समत्र (अग्रेण सहितं समयं full). निद्रया परामृश्ये - I was seized or overpowered by sleep. दुर्विकल्प - a misgiving,a wrong conception. असौ-ⅰ.. Kalpasundari. वरवर्णिनी-having an excellent complexion; a beautiful woman, विलोडन- sport in; wading through.

 P. 112. बहुभोग्या Cf. तद्युक्तं बहुमार्गगां मम पुरो निर्लज्ज वोढुं तव Ratn. I. śl between 2 and 3. \[Footnote No. 2:-In the v.1. ततस्तेन &०. तेन refers to Gajânana and मां to Bharga or Sıva (readings 8 and 10, last page). In this passage it is Śiva who speaks. विलक्षा इव - as if discomfited or abashed. वरिवस्या - service, attendance on; also, worship; fem. noun from वरिवस्यति a denom. from वरिवस्; see p. 83. अनेहस् - time. \]. भव्यः - Destined to bappen, pre-ordained. प्रियासङ्केत - मियायाः सङ्केतः the promise to meet, तस्य व्यतिकरः incident स आदो येषां तेषां स्मर॰ by thinking of such things or incidents as, &c; for व्यतिकर of. supra, pp. 29, 74. अनन्यथावृत्तिः - engaged in no other pursuit. कार्दमिकः- dyed with black paint; from फर्दम and aff. ठक् (इक); see com. Lovers are described by poets as dressing in dark in order to escape observation when proceeding on their nightly expeditions, दृढतर॰- with my loins tightly girded. प्रकृतो पस्कर-implements necessary for my purpose. By उपात्प्रतियत्नवैकृतवाक्याध्याहारेषु Pap. VI. 1. 139, a स comes before a after the prep. उप in the sense of प्रतियत्न ( गुणाधान, adding a new quality to a thing in order to enhance its value, &c.) or वैकृत (= what is prepared or altered), or वाक्याध्याहार (=sup. plying a sentence); 6. g. उपस्कृता कम्या an adorned girl, उपस्कृता ब्राह्मणाः the Brahmaņas assembled, उपस्कृतमधीते वाक्याध्याहारेण ब्रूते इत्यर्थः । Sid-Kau. In other cases we have उपकरोति. Some explain the स here by the Sūtra समवाये च Pâṇ. VI. 1. 138, 1. 6. in the sense of aggregation or collection. मातृ-his foster-mother. उद्म्भसं - filled up with water. उपखातं - near the ditch. शायितया - placed athwart the ditch (*i.e.* horizontally). स्थापिता- placed erect or vertically.

 P. 113. पक्केष्टक॰—The आ of इष्टका, इषीका and माला, and of words ending in these, is shortened when followed respectively by चित, तूल and भारिन्; a9 इष्टकचित, पक्केष्टकचितः इषीकतूलम्। मालभारि, उत्पलमालभारि. गोपुर - A city-gate with the towers attached to it, चम्पकावलि - The avenue of the Champakas. उत्तराहि-in a northerly direction. पाटलिपथ - a row of Pátali trees. स्पर्शलभ्य॰--where the bulging wall of the spacious palace could be felt. शरक्षेपमिव—nearly a bowshot's distance. पिण्डी॰— Both the sides of which were decked with the thickets of red aŚokas and jasmines. सैकतपथ - a gravel path. अवाची-lying to the south. समुद्गक-- (cylindrical) box; a lantern may have been used. निरन्तर -growing thickly. कुरण्ट -The yellow amaranth. अभिनव - marked with fresh buds rising up like hair standing on end. ०वस्तुगर्भाः- Containing or having articles in their interiors. सम्पुटाः - caskets.

 P. 114. व्यवहित - hidden. असुषीमकामा -whose passion was not cold, i. e. inflamed or intensified. कण्ठराग ०-in faltering accents, charming and impassioned. अध्यवस्य - having deliberately undertaken to do. भामिनि - O passionate (or irascible) one; भामिनी-one so delicate and sensitive to injury as to get excited at once; hence, a charminy woman also; hence used sometimes as a complimentary term; see p. 16. कदर्थिता-put to shame. He means that she was as beautiful as Rati, her eyebrows curved like Kama's bow-staff &c. Similar descriptions have already ocourred. *Cf*. pp. 2, 8 and 44. महारजन - रज्यते अनेनेति रजनं & dye महच्च तद्रजनं च; safflower. परिमलपटीयस् - having an excellent smell.

 P. 115. मञ्जुल - sweet. प्रलापैः- spontaneous utterances. पूर्णकुम्भ - is considered auspicious; see *supra* p. 44. सन्नाह्य- equipped (for gaining victory); of. जैत्ररथ pp. 3, 44. लीलाकर्ण॰- कर्णे कृतं किसलयं कर्ण, लीलार्थं कर्णकिसलयं जीवन- tending to bring back to lifo. पेशल - bewitching, charming, see com. अवसितार्थो when she reached her acme of pleasure. आरक्तवलित - reddish and rolling. अर्जरित - covered. अनर्गल — free or unrestrained. कल — indistinctly. कलकल *v. l*. is not appropriate. ०अर्पणव्यतिकराम् — bearing the red wounds of. धारणा — fixity of. अतिगूढ—deep—seated; अतिरूढ is a better reading.

 P. 116. नातिविशवदं — slightly, a faint kiss. असौ दासजन' &c. This thy slave feels life to be without purpose.' भवतंसतां &c. — Gracefully placed her folded hands on her forehead. अभिकामयमाना — herself seeking the love of. *Cf*. for a similar thought श्रिया दुरापः कथमीप्सितो भवेत् Śak. III. 13. अभिसन्धि—fixed purpose of the heart, intention. अविचारं — without troubling about (*i. e*. asking) the reason. पारं—the climax or highest perfection of. प्रागल्भ्य — proficiency, skill. आलेख्यरूप—an excellent portrait; प्रशस्तमालेख्यम्; रूप is added प्राशस्त्ये. तादृश— having that virtue. पर्वणि — Here पर्वन् means the new—moon day. विविक्ता——see com.; the first meaning is preferable. हुतमुक्ते——after the priest has worshipped the fire with offerings and retired. अगुरु — aloe-wood.

 P. 117. विसंवादः— disagreement; 'you should not fail to carry out my instructionŚ. कार्यः—शङ्क्यः *V. I*. Then tho meaning will be 'Don't you think that the rite will fail to produce the desired end.' सम्प्रधार्य— having decided upon the matter; taking counsel with. अनुमते — when they approve of it. प्रमदवनवाटी — a part of the female garden. शृङ्गाटिका — a place where four roads meet, आथर्वणिक — prescribed by the Atharva—Veda. सञ्ज्ञपित — killed. तद्धूनशमनेन—The disappearance of the smoke was to serve him as a sign. प्रगाढ — far advanced, वेतालोत्थापनं &c. — I will not raise a goblin for my own destruction. शास्त्रोपदेश —injunction of the Veda (which is not to be gainsaid). अद्भुतायमाना—coming as a wonder; prse. p. of the denom. from अद्भुत (अद्भुतायते).

  P. 118. एष विप्रलम्भो न— This cannot be a deception but अति॰. कैव कथा — Whence can there be any possibility of fraud. अभ्यूह्य— Having given duo thought or consideration to the matter. प्रौढ—pitchy or dense. गौरसर्षप — white mustard; it is one of the ingredients used in securing the efficacy of charms, etc. वसा—marrow. प्रावात्सीत् — Aor. of वस् with प्र; 'went forth in,' 'spread out'; this use of प्रवस् is rather rare; cf. प्रावसत् P. 129, where it is rightly used. निशान्तोद्यान — The garden attached to the palace. अवसित—has a double meaning— (1) The fool) has made up his mind or taken his resolve; (2) he is doomed; see note on न्यस्तशत्रिक p. 82. त्वदादिष्टया दिशा — according to your instructions (lit. by the way pointed out by you).

 P. 119. रम्भोरु — Voc. of रम्भोरू; रम्भे डव ऊरू यस्याः सा; ऊ is added in the fem. by ऊरूत्तरपदादौपम्ये Pán IV. 1. 69. रागाभि— रागः love एव अभिः शुरु — The father or the elderly person authorized to give away a girl in marriage. प्रपदेन — The forepart of the foot, the tiptoe. इतरेतरव्यतिषक्त — intertwined. विभ्रान्त — sportively turned.

  P. 120. चित्रभानु— Fire (चित्रा भानवो यस्य) उपाधि — उपधीयले आरोप्यते अनेन; उपधा+इ (कि) fraud, trick, imposition. त्वत्स्वरूपभ्रंशः —you will lose your own form and gain this; you will be transformed. भजीर्णदोषं ख्यापयेयं — I would give out that he suffered from indigestion (which led to his death). अध्यवसितं — It has been settled or determined upon. दण्डचक्रं — 8 portion of an army. पुण्ड्रदेश — a district of eastern Bengal. पाञ्चालिक, परित्रात—two proper names. खनतिनाम्रा यवनात् — Khanati may be a Persian or an Arabic trader. The name, however, sounds somewhat strange. वज्र — a diamond. गृहपतिः— The Bhûshaṇá says ग्रामाध्यक्षः; but this does not seem to be the meaning here; for he is also said to be जनपदमहत्तर; the sense of गृहपति given in the Amarakosha is a householder who helps the poor by giving them food, money—gifts &c. (सदानादिदानकर्ता). This sense will do here. Or it may be taken in the sense of the steward or the manager of the king's household;' and hence he was अन्तरङ्गभूतः (his confidant). जनपदमहत्तर —The principal man of the country; the sheriff. शलहलिः — A proper name; the man was so named probably because he was a great landlord; (शतं हलाः ploughs) यस्थ दुष्टग्रामणीः— the wicked village—leader. His wickedness lay in his being on the side of Praháravarman; see 1. 3, p. 122. जनपदकोपेन — by exciting the people of the country against him; by stirring factions against him.

 P. 121. दण्डधर — A general; or, a judge or a magistrate. उद्धारकर्मन् —the act of uprooting (destroying) him. अचिरप्रस्तुत—— lately planned or set on foot. प्रवृत्त॰—into which a great quantity of clarified butter was poured. सेवां दत्तवान् — I admitted them to my presence, *i. e*. received their homage संहतोरू—having very stout (*lit*. coming close to each other) thighs. ऊ is added to ऊरु in the fem. after संहित, शफ &c. by Pán. 1V. 1. 70. ऊरूपपीडं &c.— ऊरूम्यां भुजाम्यां चोपपीडं; for the Namuls see com.; the Namulantas of पीड्, रुध् and कष् preceded by उप are used when there is a noun in the Loc. or Instr. case used as an *Upapada*. राजकुलस्य शीलम् — the customary practices of the royal house. शुश्रूषयैव — only being devoted to his service (and not hostilely disposed towards him). दुःखमोहोपहताः —overpowered by distress and (consequent) despair. त्यक्तात्मानः — being desperate; regardless of their safety. He means If we give them the least offence, they will seize the opportunity to attack our country and fight desperately, so it will not be a wise step on our part for the present; but if we attack them when they will have a rich harvest, we shall find them unprepared for battle, throw them into confusion by destroying their crops and gain supplies for ourselves. मुष्टिवध, सस्यवध—spoiling their seed-corn or ripe harvest. See Kám. Nît. XVI. 4.

  P. 122. यात्रा— a hostile march. धर्मरक्षायै — That there should be no transgression or violation of justice righteousness. अनुगुणेन—proper or suitable. राष्ट्रमुख्य— The sheriff of the country. निनाशयिषितः — desired to be destroyed. प्रकृतिस्थे — when restored to his own dignity or position. संरम्भः—adopting hostile measures; lit. proceeding impetuously against. उद्घोषयन्तः—loudly expressing. प्रहर्षकाष्ठा॰— who had reached the highest point of ecstatic joy. अभज्ये च &c. I was at once (च) installed the Yuvaraja (crown—prince) at the command of my parents. प्रसाधितात्मा — lit. with my elevation secured; whose object was accomplished; or, according to the Com., 'with my intellect closely applied (to my work)'. देवपाद — *i.e.* of course Rajaváhana. दुर्भगान्—unpalatable, insipid, disagreeable. निर्विशन् —enjoying. अभियोग — an attack. लघुसमुत्थान — swift of foot, quickly marching. भूमि—the object of. पारतल्पिक adultery; from परतल्पः तल्प properly means 'the bed; hence, figuratively, the wife of; and aff. ठक् (इक) ०हेतुतया — This is according to the principle The end justifies the means.' दुष्टामित्र॰ — दुष्टाश्च ते अमित्राश्च; mark that the word अमित्र is *masc*. Note for the gender स्याताममित्रौ मित्रे च सहजप्राकृतावपि Sis. II. 26. प्रमापण— destruction; noun from the Cau. of मी 9 P. A'. with to destroy. अर्थधर्मौ — the comp. may also be धर्मार्थो (which is also the one more generally used) by the Várt. धर्मादिष्वनियमः *i. e*. either of the words may precede. अरीरघत् — accomplished. P. 123. स्त्रिग्धदीर्घा—स्त्रिग्धा च दीर्घा च; 'fixing upon him an affectionate and long gaze.'

 सोऽहं--I who am thus commanded. एककर्मा— having the same mission to perform, ऊर्मिमालि॰--sea-girt, bounded by the ocean. ऊर्मीणां माला अस्यास्तीति ऊर्मिभिर्मालते इति वा ऊर्मिमाली the sea. नेमि may also be taken separately. काशीपुरीङ्काशते शिवत्रिशूलोपरि अथ वा काशयति सर्वमिदम् इति काशी-इति शब्द- कल्पद्रुमः. वाराणसी—*Cf*. वरणासी च नद्यौ द्वे पुण्ये पापहरे उभे। तयोरन्तर्गता या तु सैषा वाराणसी श्रुता॥. उपस्पृश्य--having bathed. मणिकर्णिका— Name of a sacred pool (तीर्थ) at Kâsî and the ghâţa (landing-place) about it. The name is thus explained-विष्णोस्तपस्यामचयदर्शनाद्विस्मिततया शिवस्य मणिमयकुण्डलपतनादस्यास्तथात्वम्. अविमुक्तेश्वर-the lord of (अविमुक्त) Benares or Kåsiî. The name Avimukta is thus accounted for— न विमुक्तं शिवाभ्यां यदविमुक्तं ततो विदुः अन्धकमथन-- Śiva, the destroyer of the demon Andhaka; Andhaka was the son of Kâśyapa and Ditî. He is represented as a demon with two arms and heads. He was called Andhaka, as he walked like a blind man though having good sight. He was slain by Śiva when he attempted to oarry off the Pârijâta tree from heaven. According to the Matsya-P. he was about to be slain by Śiva for having attempted to carry off Pârvatî, but was admitted to the class of Ganas at his humble supplication. आयामयन्तं— very tall. आयसपरिघ— a large iron bar or rod used for locking or shutting a gate. ०परिकरं— who was girding his loins. उच्छून- swollen. कार्पण्य॰- misery. क्षीण॰— क्षीण worn away, s. e. with its animation gone, dull. साहसानुवादी— indicative of some desperate act. कृच्छ्रं— a deed of daring such as suicide. प्रपित्सते— Pres. 3rd pers. sing. of the Desid. of पढ् with प्र; wishes to do, *i. e*. is about to undertake. प्रतिपत्स्यते— *V. 1*. will also do, though the first is preferable. ०अवकाशः— room or occasion for me to render him help. सन्नाहः—preparation.

 P. 124. निर्वर्ण्य— Having closely observed. पूर्वेषु— in the Western countries. कामचरः—wandering at will. The Bhûshanâ takes इषुकाम as the name of a province, but there is no authority for it. In Vámana's Kašiká and in the Sid.- Kau. as well, on Páṇ. II. 1. 50 and on दिक्पूर्वपदादसञ्ज्ञायां ञः and प्राचां ग्रामनगराणाम् (IV. 2. 107, VII. 3. 14) we have a town mentioned, but of the name of पूर्वेषुकामशमी or इषुकामशमी; इषुकाम is not mentioned separately as the name of a province. गृहपति—may here mean 'the chief man in a village.' प्रयत्न॰—Though brought up with care. दैवच्छन्दा॰— in obedience to the will of destiny; as was fated. अर्थ—a merchant. The word originally signified ‘a cultivator of land, a peasant.' रूपाभिग्राहितः—caught with the (stolen) money on me; caught red-handed. वध्य—sentenced to death. हिंसाविहारी—delighting in killing. राजगोपुरः—The turret at the gate of the palace. उत्तम—principal . जनकण्टरव—The sounds from the throats of, i. e. the clamour of; कण्ठरव is used to match with घण्टारव. मण्डलित॰— carling his huge trunk (काण्ड) परिणमन्—when bending down to strike. दारुखण्ड—a log of wood used as a sort of stocks for the hands (of a culprit). सुषिर—holes. चण्डघट्टीत—violently struck. प्रतिमान—the part between the two tusks. नेतृ—a driver. जातसंरम्भः—exasperated, enraged. निवृत्य—turned tail. आक्रुष्टः—censured, reproached.

 P. 125. वारण— the goad. निर्याणभाग—The outer corner of the eye (as being very sensitive to pain). द्विपकिट—This vermin or pig of an elephant. निष्ठुर—peremptory. तद्विरम्य &c.—Can you therefore make up your mind to desist from this unworthy act and to follow an honourable course of life by accepting service under me? जातविश्रम्भेण—who had secured his confidence; qualifying मया ; or separately, as जातश्चासौ विश्रम्भश्च तेन—owing to the confidence he had in me रिपुञ्जयस्य— conqueror of the enemies (Rájahamsa ). विश्रुतधीः—reputed for his intellect or ability. श्रुतर्षिः—adept in the Veda; or, a sage distinguished, for his Vedic knowledge. Seven classes of Rishis are mentioned, vix. ऋषि, महर्षि, देवर्षि, ब्रह्मर्षि, परमर्षि, काण्डर्षि and श्रुतर्षि. द्वैमातुरः—born of his second or step-mother. By मातुरुत्सङ्ख्यासम्भद्र र्वायाः Pán. IV. 1. 115, मातृ, preceded by a numeral, सं and भद्र takes the aff. अण् and changes its ॠ to उद्; so द्वैमातुरः, षाण्मातुरः &c. वेश— The habitation of harlots. दुर्नय— profligacy, dissolute conduct. मदनदमन— Śiva.

 P. 126. चकमे—The use of the Perfect in the 1st pers. is a grammatical inaccuracy. छत्रं— secretly. आपन्नसत्त्वा—pregnant अपविद्धः—cast off. आरक्षिकपुरुष—the city-watch. दण्डपारुष्य—the severity of punishment. राजाज्ञया—this goes with बद्धः प्राजिहीर्ष्ये. आक्रीडनगिरि—the pleasure-mountain in the garden. यथोपपन्न— that were ready at hand or could be got at the time. प्राजिहीर्ष्ये —Was about to be killed; Imperf. Pass. of the Desid. of ह् with प्र. लूनबन्धः—The blow did not kill him, but only cut off the cords that bound him. सपरिचारा—having with her the means of service. ०त्तंसितेन—decked with her sprout-liko hands. अञ्जलिरेव किसलयः a bunch of sprouts; उत्तंसः अस्य सञ्जातः सः उत्तंसितः; अञ्जलिकितलयेनोत्तंसितः मुख॰—her curly hair way-ing about her face. अस्य मे—me in this plight or state. आभाषिता—familiarly addressed. वाङ्मय—in the form of words.परेतावास—the residence of the dead, a cemetery.

 ** P. 127.** आस्थानी—The royal court. हरसख—Kubera. वराकी—the poor girl. एतावत् &c.—the sum and substance of the whole I gathered, अभिन्न—the same in different lives. या किल &c.—Târâvali was Kâmapála's wife in more lives than one. In a previous birth she had been his wife named Aryadâsî, he being born as Śûdraka, and the mother of the boy whose re-birth from Kàntimatî had involved Kámapâla in his present trouble. This boy finally turns out to be Arthapála who is relating his account to Rájavâhana. For the easy understanding of the relationship here referred to and on the next page, 1. 12, we give it clearly below. Kâmapâla was previously born as शौनक, and शूद्रक, so the relations stand thus—

                  Name               
   Wives

**शौनक—बन्धुमती, वेदिमती, हंसावली, नन्दिनी, गोपकन्या  
शूद्रक—विनयवती, आर्यदासी, शूरसेना, रङ्गपताका, अर्थदासी  
कामपाल—कान्तिमती, सोमदेवी, सुलोचना, इन्द्रसेना, तारावली.**

 The wives are also said to be the same individuals in different lives. We get по information about Śaunaka and Śûdraka from the text. The relationship of Somadevî, Sulochanâ and Indrasená is also not directly mentioned. Perhaps they were Kâutimatî's companions in the harem. It is difficult to see what purpose is served by the mention of the different individuals here. What the context requires is that Kântimatî's son was also Târâvalî's son, being born of her as आर्यदासी in a previous life and nursed by Vinayavatî. अवर्ध्यत &o.—It appears that she was sepa-rated from the boy soon after his birth, who was therefore brought up by Vinayavatî. देव्यै वसुमत्यै &c.—See p. 19. कृतान्त—Destiny, providence, whose laws are fixed. The second कृतान्त means Yama.०दर्शित— called into existence, created.

 P. 128. प्रत्यपकृत्य—having returned the injury. निर्यातन—retaliation. प्रस्फुरन्तं—trembling. कन्याभिमर्षी— seeking union with your daughter; see p. 91. अनुवृत्त्या—The Comtakes this to mean सेवया; in this case the clause must be taken as ironical. ' I on a previous occasion violated your daughter without your अनुमति; now I have come to wipe off that offence by अनुवृति, त + त , acting according to your wishes.' But it may better be taken to mean ' by repetition of the same act of daring'; i. e. ' to wreak my vengeance on you,' as is clear from his words प्रिये प्रत्यपकृत्य &c. above. दूहितृ॰—who conferred upon me the favour or honour of accepting my daughter's hand. उत्क्रान्तसीमा—transgressing the bounds of propriety. मन्त्रिपदापदेशं—under the guise of the ministerial post; i. e. I am a mantrí in name but really a Yuvarája.स—refers to Kâmapâla. तादृशे जन्तौ—refers to Pûrṇabhadra who is relating the account to Arthapála. परिचर्यानुबन्धी—showing the favour of receiving service from me. परिचर्या सेवा तस्या अनुबन्धोऽस्यास्तीति. अलसक—consumption.

 P. 129. विधिना—properly, duly. यौवनोन्मादिन्—flushed with the pride of youth. पैशुन्यवादिनः—Insinuating something wicked. इत्थमग्राह्यत—he was persuaded to take this view of things. भुजङ्ग—a dissolute fellow, a gallant. देवज्येष्ठं—the elder brother of Your Highness.प्रकृतिवि॰—To gain the con-fidence of the subjects. क्षिणोति पुरा—पुरा gives the Present the sense of the Future. दूषितः—get against him, made to be inimically disposed towards him. यक्षिणी—Tárávalî. अयथापूर्वं—a change.देवि...भद्रे—It is strange that the Queen should address कान्ति॰ as देवि and be addressed by her as भद्रे. These two should rather be interchanged. अयथातथ्येन—by telling a lie. सत्यमनतिक्रम्य यथातथम्। तथा तथशब्दो वा सत्यार्थः। तस्य भावः यथातथ्यम्. केन—on what account. किमपि कलुषिताशया—who was offended or displeased for some reason or other. मग्दोत्रापदिष्टा addressed by my name. This misnaming is alluded to by Sanskrit poets as a constant source of trouble and offence to ladies. Cf. तस्याः स्त्रिया नामधेयेन &c. Vik. p. 29; गोत्रेषु स्खलितस्तदा भवति च व्रीडाविलक्षश्चिरम्। Śâk VI. 5; see also Kum. IV. 8. प्रणयं &c.—disregarding entreaty or supplication. उपोढमत्सरा— her jealousy increased all the more. प्रावसत्—left us. अवसीदति—is wasting away. दौर्मनस्य—दुस्थितं मनोऽस्य दुर्मनास्तस्य भावः दौर्मनस्यं mental distress or grief प्रायेण—fully; or, with exaggeration (प्राय meaning plenty, abundance).

 P.130. अथ—After that, being thus told. असौ—Siṃhaghosha. अनतिपेशलाभिः—Not very pleasing or agreeable; or not cleverly uttered; indifferent words. अबन्धयत्—threw in-to confinement स्थाने स्थाने—at every station or public centre तथोध्दरणीये &c.—His eyes are to be put out in such way that death must ensue. पुरः—before (he dies). व्यसन—peril, danger, किं तव &c.—Why should I hide it from you? सङ्कुले—in the crowd or tumult. भस्मानि हुतमिव &c.— i e. will prove fruitless. Cf.' All my efforts will end in smoke.' आशीविष—आशिषि in the jaw विषमस्य. A comp. of the पृषोदरादि class, सू being dropped and ई lengthened irregularly; or आश्यां विषमस्य according to some. अभिगृह्य—having caught or secured him यदृच्छया—accidentally. नाम—'as if;' this has here the sense of अलीक (a feigned or pretended action). तथा विषं &c.—I will so restrain or check the poison (from having a fatal effect) that he will be cast off or left with indifference, as being dead. बोधयितव्या—should be apprised or informed. अनुप्राप्त—arrived just after these events. त्वया—refers to his mother. प्रेषणीयं—a message should be sent to.

 P. 131 निरपेक्षं—Without any consideration of or regard to (his connexions &c.) गतिर्गन्तव्या—His course should be followed, i. e. should be accompanied in death. Cƒ. प्रमदाः पतिवर्त्मगा इति प्रतिपन्नं हि विचेतनैपि। Kum. IV. 33. अनुकूल— worthy of, proper for. पश्चिमो विधिः—the last rite, viz. dying with their husband काण्डपटी—canvas screen or curtain. संस्तरण—a bed. कृतानुमरणमण्डना—The woman about to immolate herself on the funeral pyr of her dead husband had to put on the dress of a Suvásinî; she had to wear a saffron-coloured garment and a similar bodice, with her hair untied, to put on her ornaments including the Mañgalasútra or the auspicious neck-ornament, and to besmear her forehead with rubbed turmeric, the symbol of her married life. बाह्यकक्षा—the outer compound. प्रवेशयिष्ये—2nd Fut. pass.

 घोषणास्थान—the place of proclamation (where his eyes were to be taken out). गुढतनुः &c.—secreted myself. यथायथं—as they were available उच्चावच—of various sorts, all manner of talk (about the culprit). उध्दुरध्वनि—adv.; 'accompanied by loud shoutings.' It may also be an adj. qualifying, महाजन the whole being one comp. word. महाजन—a great conclave of people, the mob; cf. for this meaning महाजनः स्मेरमुखो भविष्यति Kum. V. 70. It may also mean—'the principal citizens;' महाजन further on, however, means 'the mob.' मदभ्यासे— just below me उपांशु—secretly. स्थूण, अङ्गारवर्ष—Two proper names उपजप्य—having secretly instructed or prompted. उपजप्—means to make secret overtures to, to enter into a plot with.Cƒ. उपजापः कृतस्तेन तानाकोपवतस्त्वयि। Śiś. II. 99. The word occurs again at pp. 199 and 201 below. अन्धतमस—total loss of sight or blindness. Mark the ending; a final अ is added to तमस् after अव &c.; see com. न्याय्यः—i, e. is the proper punishment. प्राड्विवाक— a judge or magistrate, प्राट् च विवाकश्च प्राड्विवाकौ प्रश्नविवेकौ अस्य स्तः इति। 'विवादानुगतं पृष्ट्वा पूर्ववाक्यं प्रयत्नतः। विचारयति येनासौ प्राङ्किवाकस्ततः स्मृतः॥'

 P. 132. भीतो नाम—See com.; as if I was terrified;' or, pretending to be frightened. Cf. पतितेन नाम p 130. विहित॰—ensuring the preservation of his life. विनाचिकीर्षितः—was desired (condemned) to be deprived of दर्वीकरः—दर्वी ( the expanded hood ) एव करः यस्य a snake. रूढत्रास॰—filed away, being seized or overpowered by fear. The comp. may also be taken as रूढस्त्रासो यस्य स चासौ द्रुतश्च. ०अर्था—अर्थ the real state of things. धीरम्— steadily. कलङ्कयेयम्— I shall bring disgrace to. उत्सवोत्तरम्—उत्तर in such cases means 'conspicuous or distinguished by,' attended with, full of, &c. Cf. राज्ञा तु चरितार्थता दुःखोत्तरैव Ś' ák. V. p. 118 ; and infra उत्सवोत्तरा पुरी p. 203. मत्प्रतिषिध्द—the effect being counteracted by me. संस्थित—dead; cf. Ragh. VIII. 40.

 P. 133. मरणमण्डनं—Such ornaments and dress as a Satî usually wears. See above, note on कृतानुमरणमण्डनया p. 131. वैनतेयतां गतेन—acting the part of Garuda, i. e. in subduing the effect of the poison. Garuḍa is the principal deity invoked in the charm that nullifies the effect of serpent-poison. प्रस्नुतस्तनी—Such an effect on a mother at the sudden sight of her son is often described by Sanskrit poets. क्रूरा खल्ल तारावली—For she knew from Kubera that the foundling was then Kântimatî's, but once her own, son; and the student has seen how, instead of bringing him to Kântimatî, she took him to Vasumatî; see pp. 19 and 127. परित्यक्तः—see p. 126. उपलभ्यापि &c.— The construction is कुबेरात् तत्त्वतस्त्वामुपलभ्य. सैव वा—Mark the force of वा introducing an alternative supposition correcting a previous statement. तादृशात्—i., e. Vasumatî. भाग्यराशेः &o.—भाग्य is the result of a good store of religious merit. She means that she was not destined to enjoy the pleasure of hearing his sweet lispings, being poor in merit; but Vasumatî was, and hence Tárávali did the right thing in taking the boy to her (Vasumatî). अन्यादृशी—अन्या इव पश्यति looking like another, as if changed, so much frantic with joy she was. तथाभूतम॰—i. e. his regaining his life and son. भाग्यवन्तं—is equivalent to भाग्यवत्तरं, the sense of the comparative being expressed by the Abl. मघवतः. मनाक्—a little. प्रतिपत्तिः—What measures are we to adopt, what line of action do you mean to follow? प्राब्रवीत्—Spoke with determination or decision.

 **P. 134.** प्राकार (v. 7. M) वलय—begirt (i. e. protected) by a rampart.अक्षय्य॰— the store-house of an inexhaustible number of weapons. अलङ्घ्यतमा— impregnable. गुप्तिः—defence. अनुरुध्यन्ते—approve of, like. बाह्याभ्यन्त॰—बाह्य are the people not immediately connected with a king, such as people of the border-kingdoms, foresters &c.; अभ्यन्तरऽ are the men immediately connected with the king, such as his family-priest, the ministers, commanders of his army &c. See Kám. Nît. XVI. 19-21. कोप—disturbance, dis- affection, rebellion. प्रकृत्यमित्र—ordinary foes. अमति इति अमित्र'; fr. अम् 1 P. to go against or अम् 10 P. to afflict with sickness, +इ +व by अमोर्द्विषति चित् (Uṇâdi IV. 174). सहजान्— natural (i e. born) enemies. Enemies, like friends, are of three classes, viz. सहज or those by birth, as a paternal uncle and his Sons; प्रकृतिशत्रु or प्रकृत्यरि is one whose property or territory lies close to one's own; and कृत्रिमशत्रु
is one so by act, i. e. who has done some great harm. See Malli. on Śiś II. 36. The following verses from Kàm. Nît XXVII. will throw light on this:— अलब्धवेतनो लुब्धो मानी चाप्यवमानितः। क्रुद्धश्च कोपितोऽकस्मात्तथा भीतश्च भीषितः॥ यथाभिलषितैः कामैर्भिन्द्यदितांश्चतुर्विधान्। परपक्षे स्वपक्षे च यथावत्प्रशमं नयेत्। भेदं कुर्वीत यत्नेन मन्त्रयमात्यपुरोधसाम्॥ पौराञ्जानपदांश्चैव दण्डमुख्यांश्च दण्डवित्। साधयेद्दानभेदाभ्यां यथायोगेन चापरान्॥ दुर्दान्त— proud, insolent; or, not easily to be quelled or put down. प्रतिविधाय—having taken counter-(defensive) measures; secure against. विज्ञापितोदन्तः—informed of the affair. पारग्रामिक—Cf. supra, p. 101. Although Kâmapála was not king, yet he was treated as a hostile person besieged in his residence, and hostile measures were adopted against him, such as that of cutting off supplies, &c. प्रत्यहन्यन्त—were counteracted. उदवासितं— a house. उदूर्ध्वम् अवसीयते; fr. सो 4 P to finish, complete or सि 5 P. or 9 P. to bund, to fasten; +क्तः. गता च सा— she reached that subterranean passage. इन्दुकला—expresses loveliness, slen derness of the body &c. निर्धुनाना—entirely dispelling. विश्वम्भरा the presiding goddess of the earth. The simile expresses lofty dignity, brilliance of complexion, &c. Cf. Ragh. XV. 82, 83. हरगृहिणीव—Not a happy simile, as भवानी in her character as दुर्गा (the destroyer of demons), is fierce-looking and not gentle-looking. राजलक्ष्मीरिव &c.—Cf. for a similar idea अतिक्रान्तकुनृपतिसहस्र &c. Kád. P. 11.

 ** P. 135.** काशयष्टिः—The kâŚa plant. The kâŚa plant is chosen because it has a rich growth of white flowers, and can, therefore, fitly bear comparison with the woman having a hoary head. "This (káŚa) grass grows from ten to fifteen feet high, and the base of the flowers is surrounded with an immense quantity of bright silver-coloured wool which whitens all the fields."—Wilson. त्रासदिनं–piteously through fear. आज्ञापय—Tell us. सुदत्यः—He addresses bis speech to all the women there, as he found them frightened and confused. Mark the fem. form; शोभना दन्ता यस्याः सा सुदती a young woman. दन्त becomes दत् at the end of a Bah. when the comp. implies age or when the whole is a fem. name. See com. सत्यर्थे— there being an occasion for it. अनघ—free from any mishap or danger. सुरक्षय—the abode of the gods, heaven. अन्तर्वत्नी— big which child. A म् is added to the त् of the words अन्तर्वत् and पतिवत् before the fem. aff ई; see com. उपह्वरे—in secret. विधिवत्— in lawful wedlock; goes with . दित्सामि प्रकाशावस्थापन— rearing (lit. pla-cing) them openly. अरातिव्यसनाय— to meet calamity arising from, i. e. as a safe asylum against, an enemy. It does not mean for the incarceration of an enemy,' as there is no reason why a subterranean house was needed for that purpose; again, why were the halls comfortably built there? भूमिगृह—an under-ground mansion कृत्रिम &c.—कृत्रिमश्चासौ शैलश्च तस्य गर्भे उत्कीर्णा नाना मण्डपा यत्र तादृक् प्रेक्षागृहम्. प्रेक्षागृह— A dancing and music hall. परिबर्ह—retinue or train of attendants.

 **P. 136.** भोग्यवस्तु—articles of luxury, stores. द्व्यङ्गुलभित्तो—about two inches (fingers) deep in the wall. द्वे अङ्गुली परिमाणमस्या द्व्यङ्गुला भित्तिः, अर्धपादं— stone-covering (forming a trap-door). किष्कुविष्कम्भकं—a hand (or a span) in measure (square hand).समाः-years; the word is used generally in the pl. कामं—granted that. स्वमात्रा— her mother. प्राप्तरूपं— Here रूप is added in the sense of excellence (प्रशंसा); see supra, p.80 com.; of. also युक्तरूप Sâk. II. 16, and Râghav. on it. प्रत्युद्धृत्य—lifting it up from the opposite side. वासगृह—tha bed- D. M. 8 chamber. स्त्रेण—a multitude of women. The aff. न has the sense of 'a collection of. ' सन्दित—bound; p. p. of दो 4. P. सम् मलिन—sullen, gloomy. बिलकथा—the adventure about the subterranean cavern. निकृष्टाशय—base-hearted, vile in purpose. यथार्हेण कर्मणा—with the proper ceremonies. प्रकृति &c. —this explains why he was not set at liberty; it may also be taken with मुमुक्षितः.

** P. 137.** कृतकर्मा—कृतानि कर्माणि (services) येन; who had been serviceable before. अभियुक्त—attacked. बहूपयुक्ता—made to serve manifold purpose. ते श्वशुरः—Simhaghosha is Arthapála's father-in-law on account of his marriage with Manikarniká, the posthumous daughter of Chandaghosha, Simhaghosha's elder brother. प्रीसिस्मेरः—smiling joyfully. स्मेर is fr. स्मि + र by नमिकम्पिस्म्यजसकमहिंसदीपो रः Pán. III. 2. 167. र is added कर्तरि (in the sense of the agent) to these roots; so we get नम्र, कम्प, स्मेर, अजस्र, कम्र, हिंस्र and दीप्र.

 अभ्रङ्कष—touching or scraping the clouds. कष् takes the aff . खच् after सर्व, कूल &c. ( Pâṇ. III. 2. 42); see com. Before the aff, खच् and खश् the words अरुस्, द्विषत्, and those ending in अ, forming the object of the verb with which they are used prepo-sitionally, take the augment म्. वनस्पति—वनस्य पतिः; a tree. The स् comes in, as the word belongs to the पारस्करादी class; of. Pân.
